what country has the union jack flag: The Union Jack is the national flag of the United Kingdom, combining the crosses of England, Scotland, and Ireland.
who is zeus: Zeus is the king of the Olympian gods in Greek mythology, god of sky and thunder.
who is thor: Thor is the Norse god of thunder, storms, and strength. Son of Odin.
who is odin: Odin is the king of the Norse gods, god of wisdom, war, and death.
who is poseidon: Poseidon is the Greek god of the sea, earthquakes, and horses. Brother of Zeus.
who is hades: Hades is the Greek god of the underworld and ruler of the dead. Brother of Zeus and Poseidon.
who is apollo: Apollo is the Greek/Roman god of the sun, music, poetry, and prophecy.
who is athena: Athena is the Greek goddess of wisdom, warfare, and crafts. She sprang fully formed from Zeus's head.
who is aphrodite: Aphrodite is the Greek goddess of love, beauty, and desire. Known as Venus in Roman mythology.
who is ares: Ares is the Greek god of war. Known as Mars in Roman mythology.
who is hercules: Hercules (Heracles in Greek) is a divine hero famous for his extraordinary strength and his 12 Labours.
who is achilles: Achilles is the greatest Greek hero in the Trojan War, known for his invulnerability except for his heel.
who is odysseus: Odysseus (Ulysses in Latin) is the hero of Homer's Odyssey, known for his intelligence and 10-year journey home after the Trojan War.
what is the trojan horse: A wooden horse used by the Greeks to infiltrate the city of Troy. Soldiers hid inside and attacked from within.
what is mythology: Traditional stories of a culture's history and religion, involving gods, heroes, and supernatural beings.
who is ra: Ra is the ancient Egyptian sun god and king of the gods.
who is anubis: Anubis is the ancient Egyptian god of the dead and embalming, depicted with a jackal head.
who is isis: Isis is a major goddess in ancient Egyptian religion, goddess of magic, motherhood, and healing.
who is vishnu: Vishnu is one of the principal deities of Hinduism, the preserver and protector of the universe.
who is shiva: Shiva is one of the principal deities of Hinduism, the destroyer and transformer.
who is brahma: Brahma is the Hindu god of creation, one of the Trimurti (trinity) with Vishnu and Shiva.
who invented the telephone: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing the telephone in 1876.
who invented the lightbulb: Thomas Edison developed the first practical incandescent lightbulb in 1879. Joseph Swan developed a similar version in the UK.
who invented the internet: The internet evolved from ARPANET. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989.
who invented the airplane: The Wright Brothers (Orville and Wilbur) made the first successful powered flight on December 17, 1903.
who invented the car: Karl Benz is credited with building the first true automobile in 1885.
who invented the computer: Charles Babbage designed the first mechanical computer. Alan Turing laid theoretical foundations. The first electronic computer (ENIAC) was built in 1945.
who invented the printing press: Johannes Gutenberg invented the movable type printing press around 1440, revolutionising information sharing.
who invented the steam engine: James Watt significantly improved the steam engine in 1769, powering the Industrial Revolution.
who invented the telescope: Hans Lippershey invented the first optical telescope in 1608. Galileo improved it for astronomical use.
who invented penicillin: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928, revolutionising medicine with the first antibiotic.
who invented the radio: Guglielmo Marconi developed the first successful long-distance wireless telegraph in the 1890s.
who invented television: John Logie Baird demonstrated the first working television in 1926.
who invented the smartphone: Apple's Steve Jobs unveiled the first iPhone in 2007, transforming mobile phones into smartphones.
who invented glasses: Eyeglasses were invented around 1286 in Italy. The inventor is unknown.
who invented the vaccine: Edward Jenner developed the first vaccine in 1796 — for smallpox.
who invented dynamite: Alfred Nobel invented dynamite in 1867. He later established the Nobel Prizes.
who invented the calculator: Blaise Pascal built one of the first mechanical calculators in 1642.
who invented electricity: Electricity was not 'invented' but discovered/harnessed. Benjamin Franklin studied lightning; Michael Faraday discovered electromagnetic induction.
who invented the wheel: The wheel was invented around 3500 BC in Mesopotamia (modern Iraq).
who invented paper: Paper was invented in China around 105 AD by Cai Lun.
who invented gunpowder: Gunpowder was invented in China around the 9th century by Taoist alchemists.
who invented the compass: The magnetic compass was invented in China during the Han Dynasty (around 200 BC).
who invented zero: Zero as a number was developed in ancient India, formalised by mathematician Brahmagupta in 628 AD.
who invented algebra: Muhammad ibn Musa al-Khwarizmi, a 9th-century Persian mathematician, is considered the father of algebra.
who discovered gravity: Isaac Newton is credited with the law of universal gravitation after supposedly observing a falling apple in 1666.
who discovered penicillin: Alexander Fleming accidentally discovered penicillin in 1928 when he noticed mould killing bacteria in his lab.
who discovered america: Indigenous peoples inhabited the Americas for tens of thousands of years. Columbus arrived in 1492. Leif Erikson (Norse) reached North America around 1000 AD.
who discovered australia: Aboriginal Australians have lived on the continent for 65,000+ years. Dutch explorer Willem Janszoon made the first recorded European contact in 1606.
who split the atom: Ernest Rutherford first split the atom in 1917. The first nuclear fission of uranium was achieved by Otto Hahn and Fritz Strassmann in 1938.
what is linguistics: The scientific study of language — its structure, use, history, and relationship to culture.
how many languages are in the world: About 7,000 languages are spoken worldwide, though many are endangered.
what is the most spoken language by nati: Mandarin Chinese — with over 900 million native speakers.
what is the most widely spoken language: English — used as a first or second language by over 1.5 billion people.
what language do they speak in brazil: Portuguese — Brazil was colonised by Portugal.
what language do they speak in switzerla: Switzerland has four official languages: German, French, Italian, and Romansh.
what is latin: An ancient language of the Roman Empire. No longer spoken natively, but used in science, medicine, and law.
what is ancient greek: The language of ancient Greece (c. 800–300 BC), foundational to Western philosophy, science, and literature.
what is arabic: A Semitic language spoken by over 420 million people, primarily in the Middle East and North Africa. The language of the Quran.
what is esperanto: An artificial language created in 1887 by L. L. Zamenhof, designed to be easy to learn and promote international communication.
what is sign language: A visual language using hand shapes, facial expressions, and body movement. Different countries have their own sign languages.
what is a dialect: A regional or social variety of a language with distinct vocabulary, grammar, or pronunciation.
what is etymology: The study of the origin and history of words.
what is grammar: The set of structural rules governing the composition of sentences, phrases, and words in a language.
what is syntax: The study of how words are combined to form sentences and phrases.
what is phonetics: The study of the physical sounds of human speech.
what is a loanword: A word adopted from another language with little or no modification. E.g. 'café' (French), 'sushi' (Japanese).
what is the highest grossing film of all: Avatar (2009, re-released 2022) holds the all-time global box office record.
what is the titanic movie about: James Cameron's 1997 film depicts the tragic sinking of the RMS Titanic, framed by a fictional love story.
who directed the godfather: Francis Ford Coppola directed The Godfather (1972).
who directed star wars: George Lucas directed the original Star Wars (1977). The sequel trilogy was directed by J.J. Abrams and Rian Johnson.
what is the marvel cinematic universe: The MCU is a shared universe of superhero films and TV shows produced by Marvel Studios, based on Marvel Comics characters.
what is game of thrones: A hugely popular HBO fantasy drama series (2011–2019) based on George R.R. Martin's 'A Song of Ice and Fire' novels.
what is breaking bad: An acclaimed AMC drama series (2008–2013) about a chemistry teacher who becomes a methamphetamine manufacturer.
what is the simpsons: The world's longest-running animated TV series (1989–present), following the Simpson family in the fictional town of Springfield.
who created mickey mouse: Walt Disney created Mickey Mouse in 1928. Mickey made his debut in the animated film 'Steamboat Willie'.
what is netflix: A US streaming service founded in 1997 (originally DVD rental), now offering films, series, and documentaries globally.
what is youtube: A video-sharing platform founded in 2005, owned by Google. The world's second most visited website.
what is tiktok: A short-form video social media platform launched internationally in 2018 by Chinese company ByteDance.
what is instagram: A photo and video sharing social media platform founded in 2010, owned by Meta (Facebook).
what is twitter: A social media platform for short messages ('tweets'). Founded in 2006, rebranded as 'X' by Elon Musk in 2023.
what is facebook: The world's largest social network, founded by Mark Zuckerberg in 2004. Now part of Meta.
what is amazon: An American multinational company specialising in e-commerce, cloud computing (AWS), and digital streaming.
what is google: The world's most popular search engine, founded in 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin. Also owns YouTube, Android, and more.
what is apple company: American tech giant founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne in 1976. Makes iPhone, Mac, iPad, and more.
what is microsoft: American tech corporation founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in 1975. Makes Windows, Office, Xbox, and Azure cloud.
who is recep tayyip erdogan: Recep Tayyip Erdogan is the current President of Turkey, having held the office since 2014. He previously served as the Prime Minister of Turkey from 2003 to 2014 and is the chairman of the Justice and Development Party. Erdogan has been a dominant figure in Turkish politics for over two decades, known for his Islamist and nationalist policies.
